page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture, this server features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz with one thread per core. The memory configuration provides 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes a 67 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. No local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth reaching top-tier classifications at 391.76 GB/sec and 185.65 GB/sec respectively. Geekbench multi-core performance is also top-tier, scoring 21681.0, while single-core performance is strong. Database operations under Passmark score in the top tier at 47341.83, and Redis SET operations achieve a strong 67.07 million operations per second. However, LLM inference tasks present a performance tradeoff, with text generation for small models rated as weak, and prompt processing for large models also falling into the weak category.

Given its ARM64 architecture and high core density without hyperthreading, the c8gb.8xlarge offers high resource density and cost efficiency for parallelizable compute workloads. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by the high 67 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded decompression, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, but is less optimal for heavy LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 67 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the c8gb.8xlarge demonstrates top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core Geekbench performance, alongside strong database and static web serving capabilities. However, it exhibits weaker performance in LLM text generation and prompt processing. This profile makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized workloads, software compilation, and high-throughput database operations, while presenting a tradeoff for large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.8xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, 67 Gbps network bandwidth, and top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.8xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 67 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core ARM64 compute-optimized platform delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and high-throughput network performance for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM with top-tier memory bandwidth performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
